concerns.text: FR-008 mandates a fallback mechanism using 'aggregate population data' if birth year missingness >50%. Task T023 explicitly raises a FATAL EXCEPTION if no such source is defined, and no task exists to implement the actual fallback logic (calculating 'Global Exposure') or to define the required data source in the spec/plan. The pipeline cannot satisfy FR-008 as written.

SC-004 requires the pipeline to proceed with a warning if the match rate is below '[deferred]'. Task T036 implements a logic branch that defaults the threshold to 0.8 (80%) if the config is '[deferred]', effectively hardcoding a requirement that the spec explicitly deferred. There is no task to implement the 'proceed on low confidence' behavior without a hardcoded default.

The spec lists 'Autobiographical Memory Test (AMT)' as a data source. Task T012a requires a fatal exception if the AMT source cannot be verified by an external 'agent'. No task exists to implement this external verification agent or to define it as a runtime dependency, creating a missing implementation for a critical data integrity check.

Task T062 instructs to 'remove any other fallback URLs' if a verified source is injected. This contradicts the spec's FR-008 which requires a fallback mechanism. The task description itself contains a logical conflict with the requirement it is meant to support, indicating a stale or conflicting tag/description.
